post.summary: Submit a staged MLS membership commit to win the epoch slot

get.description: Returns commits in epoch-ascending order. Pass the current local epoch as `afterEpoch` to fetch only commits not yet applied. Clients must call this on connect and on every `commit` WS event.

X-Oldest-Retained-Epoch.description: Oldest commit epoch the server still retains for this conversation (metadata only — never the commit blob). Omitted when the conversation has no commits. A client whose local epoch is below this value has lost a pruned commit it can never fetch (sync-lost).

post.description: Atomically deletes the caller's existing device row and re-inserts it as isProvisional=false in one transaction. Eliminates the race window that a separate withdrawDevice + publishKeyPackages leaves open (a concurrent key-packages POST with a stolen bearer token could otherwise sneak in as the sole trusted device). Requires an Ed25519 proof-of-possession over the same signing key. Idempotent.

429.description: Rate limit exceeded — too many requests; retry after the `Retry-After` interval.

info.title: argus API
info.description: Crypto-blind delivery API for the argus E2EE messaging platform. Stores and forwards ciphertext only; never message content.

signaturePublicKeyPrefix.description: First 12 chars of the base64 signature public key

accessToken.description: 10-minute EdDSA JWT; store in memory only, never in storage

ceremonyId.description: Opaque ceremony ID; pass to the next step in the ceremony

accessToken.description: 10-minute EdDSA JWT; store in memory only, never in local/session storage

credential.description: base64 HMAC-SHA1 credential. SECRET-EQUIVALENT — do not log, store, or cache across calls.

iceTransportPolicy.description: "relay" in V1 — forces TURN, peer IPs never disclosed

ttlSeconds.description: Credential TTL in seconds. Client must re-fetch per call attempt.

conversationId.description: 1:1 conversation to relay the call through

media.description: V1 audio-only; video lands in V1.1

callId.description: Server-minted call identifier. Always returned (uniform 202 — no friendship/presence oracle).

relayOnly.description: If true, force TURN relay (hide peer IP). Default: true.

memberUserIds.description: other participant user ids (the caller is added automatically); 1–256

isDirect.description: true = 1:1 direct conversation; false = group. Explicit: the server cannot infer this from the initial solo member list.

clientMessageId.description: client-generated id; idempotency key per sender

ciphertext.description: opaque base64 MLS ciphertext — the server never decrypts it

attachmentObjectKey.description: optional object key of an uploaded ENCRYPTED blob (not a URL)

deduplicated.description: true if an idempotent retry matched an existing message

clientCommitId.description: client-generated id; idempotency key per sender+epoch

epoch.description: MLS epoch at which this commit was staged (server slot key)

commit.description: opaque base64 mls_private_message commit frame — server never decrypts

welcomes.description: one Welcome per added member device (max 64)

deduplicated.description: true if own idempotent retry matched an existing commit

clientCommitId.description: client-generated idempotency key for this commit

commit.description: opaque base64 mls_private_message commit frame — server never decrypts

senderUserId.description: null when the sender has exercised GDPR erasure

ciphertext.description: opaque base64 MLS ciphertext — the server never decrypts it

attachmentObjectKey.description: object key of an encrypted attachment

cursor.description: opaque keyset cursor for this message — echo as `after` to resume strictly after it (prune-safe)

nextCursor.description: LEGACY page cursor (last message id) for older clients; null when not a full page. New clients page off each per-message prune-safe cursor instead.

conversationId.description: which conversation this message belongs to

senderUserId.description: null when the sender has exercised GDPR erasure

ciphertext.description: opaque base64 MLS ciphertext — the server never decrypts it

attachmentObjectKey.description: object key of an encrypted attachment

nextCursor.description: opaque resume cursor at the last message of this page — persist it; pass as `after` to continue or resume later. Keep paging while you receive a full page. Null only on an empty page.

userId.description: the member this receipt is for

recipientUserId.description: the user being added; must be a user in this tenant

recipientDeviceId.description: the recipient's device whose claimed KeyPackage this Welcome is sealed to

welcome.description: opaque base64 MLS Welcome — the server never decrypts it

ratchetTree.description: opaque base64 MLS RatchetTree — the server never decrypts it

conversationId.description: the conversation to join with this welcome

senderUserId.description: the verified member who added you (set server-side at deliver) — resolve a display name via the directory

welcome.description: opaque base64 MLS Welcome — the server never decrypts it

ratchetTree.description: opaque base64 MLS RatchetTree — the server never decrypts it

conversationId.description: the conversation this attachment belongs to (caller must be a member)

byteSize.description: declared size of the ENCRYPTED blob in bytes

objectKey.description: server-minted opaque object key (tenant-prefixed) — reference it in the message

uploadUrl.description: short-lived presigned PUT URL — upload the ciphertext directly; never log or store it

objectKey.description: the attachment object key (NOT a URL) from the decrypted message envelope

url.description: short-lived presigned GET URL — download the ciphertext directly; never log or store it

fingerprint.description: Public device fingerprint (QR/code) displayed by D2

deviceId.description: D2's own server device UUID (from POST /devices/me/key-packages)

approverSignaturePublicKey.description: D1's registered signature public key (base64) — used by D2 to verify D1's key package

proof.description: base64url Ed25519 enroll-approval proof from D1

signaturePublicKey.description: The device's Ed25519 signature public key (base64) — identifies which device to withdraw
The device's Ed25519 signature public key (base64) — the key being migrated to a composite identity

isDirect.description: true = direct 1:1; false = group; null = pre-migration (unknown)

peerUserId.description: Peer user ID for DMs; null for groups, in-flight solo convs, and GDPR-erased peers

conversations.description: Conversations the caller is a member of (metadata only)

conversationIds.description: Backward-compat shim for stale PWA bundles that read .conversationIds. Remove once the new client version is confirmed shipped.

argusId.description: Exact argus-id of the user to befriend

status.description: Constant — carries no outcome (no enumeration oracle)

displayName.description: new display name: 2–32 chars after trimming; letters, numbers, spaces, and . _ - ' only

avatarSeed.description: aesthetic seed token for deterministic avatar generation (≤64 chars)

endpointPrefix.description: first 40 chars of the push endpoint URL (service identification only)

metadata.description: non-sensitive metadata IDs only — never content or keys

otherUserId.description: the other party in the friendship/request

direction.description: set for pending requests only (null once accepted)

published.description: net-new KeyPackages inserted by this call

available.description: total unclaimed KeyPackages for this device after the call

isProvisional.description: true when this device must still be approved by an existing trusted device

signaturePublicKey.description: base64 MLS signature public key — verify its fingerprint out-of-band
base64 MLS signature public key identifying the caller's own device

revoked.description: unclaimed KeyPackages revoked (deleted) for the caller's device

token.description: One-time plaintext token — returned once, never stored
